target.breakdown: The universal Spanish greeting, used any time of day.
Ask how someone is doing informally.
Returns the question back to the speaker.
Literally 'of nothing' — the standard response to gracias.
Excuse me (to get attention or apologise).
The most common way to ask someone's name.
Ask about manner, or to ask someone to repeat.
Use son las + number for most hours.
Use es la una for 1 o'clock only.

target.text: La reunión empieza a las diez
target.romanization: lah reh-oo-NYOHN em-PYEH-sah ah lahs DYEHS

scenarios.title: Ordering at a Tapas Bar
scenarios.description: You are in a lively tapas bar in Seville and the waiter is heading over.

phrasebook.english: I'd like a draught beer, please.
